request_summary: Apply analyst Phase 1 to Phase 2 orchestration fixes so JARVIS reinvokes temper-analyst for spec generation instead of stopping after PRD

context.notes: Requested fix: keep analyst Phase 1 and Phase 2 orchestration behavior, and continue slimming temper-jarvis.agent.md by moving agent-specific delegation contracts, including implementation-agent handoff rules, into dedicated workflow skills loaded only when needed.

approved_plan.description: Apply the analyst Phase 1 to Phase 2 orchestration fixes across JARVIS and its workflow skills
approved_plan.output: Updated JARVIS so temper-analyst remains two explicit invocations, analyst and architect operating contracts live in dedicated workflow skills, and implementation-agent delegation detail now lives in a dedicated implementation-delegation workflow skill instead of bloating temper-jarvis.agent.md.

approved_plan.description: Verify the new analyst Phase 1 and Phase 2 orchestration flow for consistency across JARVIS and state contracts
approved_plan.output: Verification passed for the refactor itself: JARVIS is now more focused and the analyst/architect/implementation-agent contracts are properly split into workflow skills. Remaining issues: old temper-orchestrator and orchestrator-state naming still exists in README, temper-plan, temper-next, and temper-status; pre-refactor analyst cycles without explicit phase would fall back to recovery rather than seamless resume.

completed_tasks.title: Apply analyst Phase 1 to Phase 2 orchestration fixes
Verify analyst Phase 1 and Phase 2 orchestration consistency

current_output: Plan complete. JARVIS now keeps specialist operating contracts in dedicated workflow skills instead of carrying them all in temper-jarvis.agent.md. Verification passed, with two remaining follow-up items: stale temper-orchestrator/orchestrator-state references in a few docs and no seamless migration path for legacy analyst cycles without explicit phase state.
